data.body:
[G3 child-coverage audit — parent 72d5 (Migrate the LLM gates onto the workflow engine) vs child B1 (Production batch-runner — extract sizing.py behind epic A's BatchRunner seam)] Clean — no findings.

4-bucket audit of parent criteria against this child, with whole-roster absence cross-check:
- Parent AC1 (plan-review as engine workflow + production batch-runner extracted from sizing.py behind epic A's seam): PARTIALLY covered by B1 — it delivers exactly the "production batch-runner extracted from sizing.py behind epic A's seam" sub-clause (ProductionBatchRunner adapting pass1_with_ladder/shed_to_budget/plan_budget_cap/MODEL_LADDER/checkpoint to BatchRunRequest→BatchRunResult, opaque-plan journaling). Workflow-expression remainder FULLY covered by sibling B2 (6b06), which explicitly drives its batch step with B1's runner. No gap.
- Parent AC2 (verify_completion as engine workflow): structural/not-this-child — covered by sibling B3 (dc58).
- Parent AC3 (planned-trace parity harness, offline, CI): not-this-child — covered by sibling B4 (101a).
- Parent AC4 (cutover behind gate; close/claim intact): B1 explicitly defers ("cutover is B5") — covered by siblings B5 (f12d) + 0408.
- Parent AC5 (remove sample stubs; preserve shims; retain demo): not-this-child — covered by sibling B6 (7e48).
- Parent AC6 (end state: two real workflows + one sample; lint+tests pass): not-this-child — covered by sibling B6 (7e48); B1's own lint/test-suite AC contributes the lint/tests portion.
- Parent success criterion (both gates on ONE engine substrate; prompts in engine prompt library; offline parity; only real workflows remain): B1 contributes the batch-runner substrate enabling plan-review Pass-1 to run as an engine `batch` step; remainder covered by B2/B3/B4/B6 (+ B-DM 5506 for the prompt/criteria library).

Every parent acceptance/success criterion is either covered by this child (the batch-runner half of AC1) or fully covered by a named sibling in the roster. No uncovered parent criterion attributable to this pairing.
